avm.title: First Own Apartment
avm.desc: Learn Excel basics with everyday tasks

exercise.skipConfirm: Really skip? You can try again later!
exercise.formulaHint: SUM = SUMME, AVERAGE = MITTELWERT — both work!

level1.title: Shopping
level1.story: You are moving into your first apartment! First you need a shopping list. Let's use Excel!

ex1.title: Shopping List
ex1.story: Write your first shopping list in a spreadsheet.
ex1.hint1: Click on cell A2 and type 'Rice'
ex1.hint2: Cells are like a grid – A is the column, 2 is the row

ex2.title: Enter Prices
ex2.story: Now let's enter the prices next to the items.
ex2.step1: Enter the price 1.99 in cell B2
ex2.step2: Enter the price 0.89 in cell B3
ex2.step3: Enter the price 2.49 in cell B4
ex2.step4: Enter the price 0.59 in cell B5
ex2.hint1: Click on B2 and type the number 1.99
ex2.hint2: Use a period as decimal separator (e.g. 1.99)

ex3.title: Calculate Sum
ex3.story: How much does everything cost? Use your first formula!
ex3.step2: Write the formula =SUM(B2:B5) in cell B6
ex3.hint1: A formula always starts with an equals sign =
ex3.hint2: SUM() adds all numbers in the range. B2:B5 means from B2 to B5.

ex4.title: Check Budget
ex4.story: You have 10 euros. Is that enough?
ex4.step4: Write the formula =B8-B6 in cell B9
ex4.hint1: B8 is your budget, B6 is the sum of your purchases
ex4.hint2: Start a formula with =. The minus sign - subtracts.

level2.title: My Daily Life
level2.story: Your first week in the new apartment! Organize your daily life with Excel.

ex1.title: Weekly Planner
ex1.story: Plan your first week in the new apartment.
ex1.step4: Enter an activity for Monday in B2

ex2.title: Price Comparison
ex2.step1: Enter the Aldi price 1.29 in cell B2
ex2.step2: Enter the Rewe price 1.59 in cell C2
ex2.step3: Enter the Aldi price 0.79 in cell B3
ex2.step4: Enter the Rewe price 0.99 in cell C3
ex2.hint1: Column B is for Aldi, column C is for Rewe
ex2.hint2: Look carefully which store is cheaper!

ex3.title: Monthly Expenses
ex3.story: Keep track of your monthly expenses.
ex3.step1: Enter 350 for rent in cell B2
ex3.step2: Enter 150 for food in cell B3
ex3.step3: Enter 30 for phone in cell B4
ex3.step4: Calculate the sum with =SUM(B2:B5) in cell B6
ex3.hint1: These numbers are your monthly costs in euros
ex3.hint2: SUM automatically adds all values in the range

ex4.title: Calculate Average
ex4.story: What is your average spending per category?
ex4.step4: Calculate the average with =AVERAGE(B2:B4) in cell B6
ex4.hint1: AVERAGE calculates the mean of all numbers
ex4.hint2: The average is the sum divided by the count

level3.title: Cooking & Recipes
level3.story: You are cooking for yourself now! Plan recipes, shopping and calories with Excel.

ex1.title: Scale a Recipe
ex1.story: A recipe is for 2 people. But you have 4 guests! Scale the quantities.
ex1.step1: Enter the quantities: 200g pasta (B2), 100g cheese (B3), 2 eggs (B4), 150ml cream (B5)
ex1.step2: Enter the factor 2 in cell C2 (for double quantity)
ex1.step3: Calculate the new quantity for pasta: =B2*C2 in cell D2
ex1.step4: Calculate also cheese (D3), eggs (D4) and cream (D5) with the same formula
ex1.hint1: The asterisk * is the multiplication sign in Excel
ex1.hint2: Copy the formula: D3 needs =B3*C3, D4 needs =B4*C4

ex2.title: Weekly Menu
ex2.story: Plan your meals for the week and calculate the costs per day.
ex2.step1: Enter Monday's costs: Breakfast 2 (B2), Lunch 5 (C2), Dinner 4 (D2)
ex2.step2: Calculate the daily costs for Monday: =SUM(B2:D2) in cell E2
ex2.step3: Enter Tuesday's costs and calculate daily costs (E3)
ex2.step4: Calculate weekly costs: =SUM(E2:E6) in cell E8
ex2.hint1: Each row is a day, each column is a meal
ex2.hint2: SUM(B2:D2) adds all meals for one day

ex3.title: Calorie Tracker
ex3.story: Count your calories for a day and compare with your daily goal.
ex3.step1: Enter the calories: Breakfast 400 (B2), Lunch 600 (B3), Dinner 500 (B4), Snacks 200 (B5)
ex3.step2: Calculate total calories: =SUM(B2:B5) in cell B7
ex3.step3: Enter your daily goal 2000 in cell B8
ex3.step4: Calculate remaining calories: =B8-B7 in cell B9
ex3.hint1: SUM adds all calories for the day

ex4.title: Recipe Shopping
ex4.story: Calculate the costs for all ingredients of your favorite recipe.
ex4.step1: Enter price and quantity: Chicken 6.99 (B2), 1 piece (C2)
ex4.step2: Calculate total price for chicken: =B2*C2 in cell D2
ex4.step3: Calculate also rice (D3), vegetables (D4) and spices (D5)
ex4.step4: Calculate shopping total: =SUM(D2:D5) in cell D7
ex4.hint1: Total price = price per piece times quantity
ex4.hint2: Use a period as decimal separator (e.g. 6.99)

level4.title: Shared Apartment & Finances
level4.story: You now live in a shared apartment! Split costs, plan savings and keep track.

ex1.title: Split Utility Bills
ex1.story: The utility bills need to be split fairly between 2 roommates.
ex1.step1: Enter the costs: Electricity 80 (B2), Water 40 (B3), Internet 30 (B4)
ex1.step2: Calculate each person's share for electricity: =B2/2 in cell C2
ex1.step3: Calculate also water (C3) and internet (C4) per person
ex1.step4: Calculate total per person: =SUM(C2:C4) in cell C6
ex1.hint1: The slash / is the division sign in Excel
ex1.hint2: Each person pays half: amount divided by 2

ex2.title: Savings Plan
ex2.story: You want to save 600 for a new bicycle. Create a savings plan!
ex2.step2: Calculate the duration in months: =B2/B3 in cell B4
ex2.step3: Enter what you have already saved: Months 1-3 at 50 each (B7, B8, B9)
ex2.step4: Calculate Total saved =SUM(B7:B9) in B10, Remaining =B2-B10 in B11
ex2.hint1: Duration = savings goal divided by monthly rate
ex2.hint2: Remaining = savings goal minus already saved

ex3.title: Calculate Discount
ex3.story: The furniture store has a sale! Calculate the final prices after discount.
ex3.step1: Enter prices and discounts: Lamp 40 (B2), 20% discount (C2)
ex3.step2: Calculate the savings for the lamp: =B2*C2/100 in cell D2
ex3.step3: Calculate the final price: =B2-D2 in cell E2. Do the same for carpet and shelf.
ex3.step4: Calculate total final price: =SUM(E2:E4) in cell E6
ex3.hint1: Savings = price times discount divided by 100
ex3.hint2: Final price = original price minus savings

ex4.title: Monthly Overview
ex4.story: Create a complete monthly overview with income, expenses and balance.
ex4.step1: Enter your income: Training allowance 800 (B2), Child benefit 250 (B3)
ex4.step2: Calculate Total income =SUM(B2:B3) in B4, Total expenses =SUM(B7:B10) in B11
ex4.step3: Calculate the balance (income minus expenses): =B4-B11 in cell B13
ex4.step4: Find the highest expense =MAX(B7:B10) in B14 and the lowest =MIN(B7:B10) in B15
ex4.hint1: MAX finds the largest value, MIN the smallest
ex4.hint2: Balance = what comes in minus what goes out

level5.title: Class Trip
level5.story: Your class is planning a class trip! Calculate costs, distribute rooms and settle accounts at the end.

ex1.title: Travel Costs
ex1.story: Calculate the total costs of the class trip for 25 students.
ex1.step1: Enter the participant count 25 in all quantity cells (C2 to C5)
ex1.step2: Calculate total costs for bus: =B2*C2 in cell D2
ex1.step3: Calculate also accommodation (D3), meals (D4) and museum (D5)
ex1.step4: Total costs =SUM(D2:D5) in D7, cost per person =D7/C2 in D8
ex1.hint1: Total cost = price per person times number of participants
ex1.hint2: Cost per person = total costs divided by number of participants

ex2.title: Pocket Money
ex2.story: You have 60 euros pocket money for 5 days. Keep track!
ex2.step1: Enter your daily expenses: Mon 8, Tue 12, Wed 5, Thu 15, Fri 10 (B2 to B6)
ex2.step2: Calculate total expenses: =SUM(B2:B6) in cell B8
ex2.step3: Calculate daily average: =AVERAGE(B2:B6) in cell B9
ex2.step4: Enter budget 60 in B11, calculate remaining =B11-B8 in B12, percentage =B8/B11*100 in B13
ex2.hint1: AVERAGE calculates the mean across all days
ex2.hint2: Percentage spent = expenses divided by budget times 100

ex3.title: Room Distribution
ex3.story: Distribute the class across different room types and calculate the costs.
ex3.step1: Enter: 3 nights (C2-C4), 2 single rooms (D2), 5 double rooms (D3), 3 triple rooms (D4)
ex3.step2: Calculate costs for single rooms: =B2*C2*D2 in cell E2
ex3.step3: Calculate also double rooms (E3) and triple rooms (E4)
ex3.step4: Total rooms =SUM(D2:D4) in E6, total costs =SUM(E2:E4) in E7, avg costs =E7/E6 in E8, cheapest =MIN(B2:B4) in E9
ex3.hint1: Cost = price per night times nights times number of rooms
ex3.hint2: Average cost = total costs divided by number of rooms

ex4.title: Settlement
ex4.story: After the trip: who paid how much? Calculate the differences.
ex4.step1: Enter how much each person paid: Anna 120, Ben 45, Clara 80, David 55 (B2-B5)
ex4.step2: Calculate Total =SUM(B2:B5) in B7, fair share =B7/4 in B8
ex4.step3: Enter the fair share =B$8 in C2, calculate difference =B2-C2 in D2 (also for B3-B5)
ex4.step4: Highest contribution =MAX(B2:B5) in B10, lowest =MIN(B2:B5) in B11, range =B10-B11 in B12
ex4.hint1: Positive = gets money back, negative = still needs to pay

level6.title: Pro Spreadsheets
level6.story: You are almost an Excel pro! Solve complex tasks with nested formulas and percentage calculations.

ex1.title: Grade Calculator
ex1.story: Calculate your grade average and analyze your performance.
ex1.step1: Enter grades and scores: Math 2/85%, German 3/72%, English 1/91%, Business 3/68%, IT 2/80%
ex1.step3: Best grade =MIN(B2:B6) in B9, worst =MAX(B2:B6) in B10, range =B10-B9 in B11
ex1.step4: Calculate avg score =AVERAGE(C2:C6) in C13, max score =MAX(C2:C6) in C14
ex1.hint1: For grades, MIN is best (1) and MAX is worst (6)
ex1.hint2: The grade range shows how varied your performance is

ex2.title: Salary Calculator
ex2.story: Calculate your wages as a trainee – from hourly rate to net pay.
ex2.step2: Calculate weekly wage =B2*B3 in B6, monthly wage =B6*B4 in B7
ex2.step3: Calculate annual wage =B7*12 in B8, social contributions =B7*0.2 in B10, net =B7-B10 in B11
ex2.step4: Calculate net/day =B11/22 in B13 (22 working days), net/hour =B11/B3/B4 in B14
ex2.hint2: Net = gross minus deductions. 22 working days per month is standard.

ex3.title: Inventory
ex3.story: Take inventory for your small online shop. Calculate values and profits.
ex3.step1: Enter: T-Shirts cost 8/price 20/50 pcs, Caps 5/15/30, Mugs 3/10/80, Stickers 1/5/200
ex3.step2: Calculate profit per piece: =C2-B2 in E2, likewise for all products
ex3.step3: Total stock =SUM(D2:D5) in D7, avg profit =AVERAGE(E2:E5) in E8, best profit =MAX(E2:E5) in E9
ex3.step4: Inventory value cost =SUM(B2*D2,B3*D3,B4*D4,B5*D5) in E11, sales price analog in E12, potential profit =E12-E11 in E13
ex3.hint1: Profit per piece = selling price minus purchase price
ex3.hint2: Inventory value = sum of (price times stock) for each product

ex4.title: Year Comparison
ex4.story: Compare this year's revenue with last year – per quarter and total.
ex4.step1: Enter this year's revenue: Q1 1400, Q2 1650, Q3 1900, Q4 2200 (C2-C5)
ex4.step2: Calculate the difference per quarter: =C2-B2 in D2, for all quarters
ex4.step3: Calculate the percentage change: =D2/B2*100 in E2, for all quarters
ex4.step4: Annual total last year =SUM(B2:B5) in B7, this year =SUM(C2:C5) in C7, avg quarter =AVERAGE(C2:C5) in C8, best quarter =MAX(C2:C5) in C9, total difference =C7-B7 in D12, total growth =D12/B7*100 in E12
ex4.hint1: Percentage change = difference divided by last year's value times 100
ex4.hint2: A positive value means growth, a negative value means decline

level1.title: Mastering Cell References
level1.story: Internship Day 1 at Schulz & Partner GmbH – a wholesale office supply company. Your task: update the price list using absolute cell references.

ex1.title: Calculate VAT
ex1.story: You need to calculate the gross prices for all items. The VAT factor 1.19 is stored in cell C1 and applies to all products.
ex1.step1: Enter the formula =B2*$C$1 in cell D2 to calculate the gross price for the folder
ex1.step2: Copy the formula to D3 – note: $C$1 stays fixed, B3 adjusts automatically
ex1.step3: Calculate the gross prices for pens (D4) and glue (D5) as well
ex1.step4: Verify: all formulas in D2:D5 must reference $C$1
ex1.hint1: The dollar sign $ makes a reference absolute – it does not change when copied
ex1.hint2: =B2*$C$1 means: net price from B2 times the fixed VAT factor from C1

ex2.title: Currency Conversion
ex2.story: A US customer wants to see prices in USD. The EUR/USD exchange rate is stored in cell E1.
ex2.step1: Enter the formula =B2*$E$1 in cell C2 to calculate the USD price for copy paper
ex2.step2: Copy the formula to C3 – the exchange rate in $E$1 stays fixed
ex2.step3: Calculate the USD prices for the printer (C4) and laptop (C5) as well
ex2.step4: Verify: all formulas in C2:C5 must use $E$1 as the fixed exchange rate
ex2.hint1: $E$1 locks both column E and row 1 – the reference never changes
ex2.hint2: USD price = EUR price multiplied by the exchange rate

ex3.title: Discount Tiers
ex3.story: Schulz & Partner offers two customer groups different discounts: Standard (10%) and Premium (20%). Calculate the net prices after the discount.
ex3.step1: Enter =B3*(1-C$2) in C3 – the discount rate in row 2 is locked, but the column can change
ex3.step2: Copy the formula to C4, C5 and C6 for all items (Standard discount)
ex3.step3: Enter =B3*(1-D$2) in D3 for the Premium price of the first item
ex3.step4: Copy the Premium formula to D4, D5 and D6 as well
ex3.hint1: C$2 locks only the row (2), not the column – so the formula can be copied horizontally
ex3.hint2: (1-discount) gives the factor: 1-0.1 = 0.9 means 90% of the original price

ex4.title: Calculate Commission
ex4.story: The sales team earns a 5% commission on revenue. The commission rate is stored in C1 and applies to all salespeople.
ex4.step1: Enter =B2*$C$1 in D2 to calculate the commission for Müller, A.
ex4.step2: Copy the formula to D3 for Schmidt, B.
ex4.step3: Calculate the commission for Weber, C. (D4) and Fischer, D. (D5) as well
ex4.step4: Note: without the $ signs, C1 would shift to C2, C3 etc. when copying – that would be wrong!
ex4.hint1: $C$1 ensures the commission rate from C1 is always used

level6.title: Month 5: Sales Analysis
level6.story: You have opened your own online shop! Time to analyse your sales data using SUMIF and COUNTIF.

ex1.title: Revenue by Category
ex1.story: Find out how much revenue each product category generates — SUMIF lets you sum selectively by category.
ex1.step2: Calculate revenue for Buerobedarf in B13: =SUMIF(B2:B9,"Buerobedarf",C2:C9)
ex1.step3: Calculate revenue for Moebel in B14: =SUMIF(B2:B9,"Moebel",C2:C9)
ex1.step4: Check: Electronics = 479.96 EUR, Office supplies = 77.50 EUR, Furniture = 449.00 EUR
ex1.hint1: SUMIF(range,criterion,sum_range): column B holds the categories, column C the amounts
ex1.hint2: The criterion text must match the cell content exactly — case-insensitive

ex2.title: Counting Orders
ex2.story: Analyse how many orders meet certain conditions — COUNTIF counts cells matching a criterion.
ex2.step1: Count orders above 50 EUR in B12: =COUNTIF(C2:C9,">50")
ex2.step2: Count orders below 50 EUR in B13: =COUNTIF(C2:C9,"<50")
ex2.step4: Check: >50 EUR = 6 orders, <50 EUR = 2 orders, Electronics = 4 orders
ex2.hint1: COUNTIF(range,criterion): comparison operators like >50 must be inside quotation marks
ex2.hint2: Both text criteria (e.g. "Elektronik") and numeric comparisons (e.g. ">50") need quotation marks

ex3.title: Branch Comparison
ex3.story: Your shop has three branches! Compare revenue and order count per branch using SUMIF and COUNTIF.
ex3.hint1: The branch is in column B, the amount in column D — adjust the sum_range accordingly
ex3.hint2: Hamburg = 578.99 EUR (3 orders), Berlin = 305.48 EUR (3), Munich = 341.99 EUR (3)

ex4.title: Finding Top Customers
ex4.story: Who are your best customers? Calculate total revenue, order count and average order value per customer.
ex4.step1: Calculate total revenue for Firma A in B12: =SUMIF(B2:B9,"Firma A",C2:C9)
ex4.step2: Count Firma A orders in C12: =COUNTIF(B2:B9,"Firma A")
ex4.step4: Fill in the formulas for Firma B (row 13) and Firma C (row 14) as well
ex4.hint1: Average = total revenue ÷ number of orders. Use the calculated cells B12 and C12
ex4.hint2: Firma A: 1,000 EUR, 3 orders, avg 333.33 EUR. Firma B: 730 EUR, 3 orders, avg 243.33 EUR

level5.title: Month 4: Supplier Comparison
level5.story: Month 4 at Schulz & Partner. You have mastered VLOOKUP — now you combine it with IF, MIN and IFERROR for real business decisions.

ex1.title: Best Price
ex1.story: Three suppliers, one product — who is cheapest? Use VLOOKUP for each supplier and MIN for the best price.
ex1.step1: Enter in B2: =VLOOKUP(A2,Stammdaten!A:D,2,0) for Supplier 1 price
ex1.step2: Enter in C2: =VLOOKUP(A2,Stammdaten!A:D,3,0) for Supplier 2 price
ex1.step3: Enter in D2: =VLOOKUP(A2,Stammdaten!A:D,4,0) for Supplier 3 price
ex1.step4: Calculate the best price in E2: =MIN(B2,C2,D2)
ex1.hint1: VLOOKUP returns the value from a specific column — column index 2, 3 or 4
ex1.hint2: MIN(B2,C2,D2) automatically finds the smallest of the three prices

ex2.title: Reorder Proposal
ex2.story: Check stock levels via VLOOKUP and decide with IF whether to reorder.
ex2.step1: Fetch the stock with VLOOKUP: =VLOOKUP(A2,Stammdaten!A:D,3,0) in B2
ex2.step2: Fetch the minimum stock: =VLOOKUP(A2,Stammdaten!A:D,4,0) in C2
ex2.step3: Create the reorder proposal: =IF(B2<C2,"Nachbestellen","OK") in D2
ex2.step4: Copy all three formulas for the remaining articles in rows 3 and 4
ex2.hint1: Column index 3 = stock, column index 4 = minimum stock in the reference table

ex3.title: Complete Customer Invoice
ex3.story: Create a full invoice: name and price via VLOOKUP, total price including VAT via formula.
ex3.step3: Calculate the total including VAT: =C2*D2*(1+$E$1) in F2
ex3.step4: Copy all formulas for the remaining articles (rows 3 and 4)
ex3.hint1: $E$1 locks the VAT cell — it stays on E1 when copying
ex3.hint2: Total = price × quantity × (1 + VAT). With 19% VAT: × 1.19

ex4.title: Error Detection
ex4.story: Some article numbers are wrong! Protect your formula with IFERROR — unknown numbers should show 'Nicht gefunden'.
ex4.step3: Copy both formulas to row 3 (article X-9999 — this does not exist!)
ex4.step4: Copy the formulas to rows 4 and 5 as well
ex4.hint1: IFERROR(formula,error_value): if VLOOKUP finds nothing (#N/A), the error value is shown
ex4.hint2: X-9999 and X-8888 are invalid numbers — they should display 'Nicht gefunden'

level3.title: Month 2: Customer Evaluation
level3.story: In your second month at Schulz & Partner you analyse customer data and make complex business decisions using IF+AND/OR.

ex1.title: Customer Status
ex1.story: Classify customers as Premium or Standard — but only when BOTH criteria are met.
ex1.step4: Copy the formula to rows 5 and 6 as well
ex1.hint1: AND() means: ALL conditions must be true at the same time
ex1.hint2: Premium = revenue above 5,000 EUR AND more than 3 orders

ex2.title: Express Shipping
ex2.story: Determine the shipping method — high weight OR high value triggers freight forwarding.
ex2.step4: Copy the formula to rows 5 and 6 as well
ex2.hint1: OR() means: AT LEAST ONE condition must be true
ex2.hint2: Freight = over 20 kg OR value over 500 EUR

ex3.title: Discount Class
ex3.story: Calculate the discount rate for each customer — using nested IF functions for three tiers.
ex3.step4: Copy the formula to rows 5 and 6 as well

ex4.title: Supplier Rating
ex4.story: Rate suppliers A, B or C — combine AND and OR in nested IF formulas.
ex4.hint2: B = Price >= 5 OR Quality >= 5 (when no A applies). C = both below 5

level4.title: Month 3: Inventory Management
level4.story: Month 3 at Schulz & Partner. You manage the product database and need to look up information instantly. With VLOOKUP you get exactly the data you need!

ex1.title: Look Up Article Price
ex1.story: The boss needs the current prices for four articles right away. Use VLOOKUP to find the prices from the master data table.
ex1.step1: Look at the Stammdaten sheet: column A = article number, column B = description, column C = price
ex1.step3: Copy the formula to B3 and B4
ex1.step4: Complete B5 as well — check that all four prices are displayed correctly
ex1.hint1: VLOOKUP(search_value,range,column,0) — the 3 means: return the value from the 3rd column
ex1.hint2: The 0 at the end means exact match. Stammdaten!A:C means: search in the 'Stammdaten' sheet

ex2.title: Find Supplier
ex2.story: For a reorder you need to quickly find out which supplier delivers which article. Use VLOOKUP on the supplier column.
ex2.step1: Look at the Stammdaten sheet: now there are 4 columns — A = article number, B = description, C = price, D = supplier
ex2.step3: Copy the formula to B3 and B4
ex2.step4: Complete B5 as well and check all supplier names
ex2.hint1: The 4 refers to the 4th column in the lookup range — that is the supplier column
ex2.hint2: Compare with exercise 1: there it was column 3 (price), here it is column 4 (supplier)

ex3.title: Create Invoice
ex3.story: You are creating a purchase order. Enter description and price via VLOOKUP and then calculate the total amount.
ex3.step1: Look at the task: article number and quantity are already filled in. You need description, price and total.
ex3.step2: Enter in B2: =VLOOKUP(A2,Stammdaten!A:C,2,0) for the description
ex3.step3: Enter in C2: =VLOOKUP(A2,Stammdaten!A:C,3,0) for the price. Copy both formulas downward.
ex3.step4: Calculate the total: enter =C2*D2 in E2 and copy it downward
ex3.hint1: For description: column 2 in VLOOKUP. For price: column 3. Both from the same lookup range.
ex3.hint2: Total = price × quantity. When price is calculated via VLOOKUP, =C2*D2 works the same way.

ex4.title: Determine Storage Location
ex4.story: In the warehouse you need to locate 3 articles. Use VLOOKUP three times per row — for warehouse, shelf and compartment.
ex4.step1: Look at Stammdaten: column C = warehouse, D = shelf, E = compartment
ex4.step2: Enter in B2: =VLOOKUP(A2,Stammdaten!A:E,3,0) for the warehouse
ex4.step3: Enter in C2: =VLOOKUP(A2,Stammdaten!A:E,4,0) for the shelf, and in D2: =VLOOKUP(A2,Stammdaten!A:E,5,0) for the compartment
ex4.step4: Copy all three formulas for rows 3 and 4
ex4.hint1: Warehouse = column 3, shelf = column 4, compartment = column 5 — always from Stammdaten!A:E
ex4.hint2: All three VLOOKUP formulas search in the same table — only the column number changes

level2.title: Making Decisions
level2.story: Week 2 at Schulz & Partner. You are now taking over invoice checking. With the IF function you make decisions automatically!

ex1.title: Payment Status Check
ex1.story: Check the open invoices: which ones are already paid, which are still outstanding?
ex1.step1: Look at the table: column A contains invoice numbers, column B the outstanding amount.
ex1.step3: Copy the formula for invoices C3 to C5
ex1.step4: Check C6: enter the IF formula there as well
ex1.hint1: IF checks a condition: IF(condition,then,else). If the amount is 0, the invoice is paid.
ex1.hint2: Copy the formula downward: click on C2, then drag the small square in the corner downward.

ex2.title: Cash Discount Calculation
ex2.story: Schulz & Partner grants a 2% cash discount if payment is made within 10 days. Calculate the discount amount!
ex2.step1: Look at the table: column B shows the invoice amount, column C the days until payment.
ex2.step3: Copy the formula for rows D3 to D5
ex2.step4: Complete the formula in D6 as well
ex2.hint1: The cash discount only applies when payment is made within 10 days. Otherwise the discount is 0.
ex2.hint2: B2*0.02 equals 2% of B2. The period here is the decimal separator.

ex3.title: Stock Alert
ex3.story: Stock levels must be monitored. Items with fewer than 10 units must be reordered!
ex3.step1: Look at the table: column A contains the items, column B the current stock level.
ex3.step3: Copy the formula for C3 to C5
ex3.step4: Complete C6 with the IF formula as well
ex3.hint1: The comparison operator < means 'less than'. If stock < 10 the item must be reordered.
ex3.hint2: Text values in formulas are written in quotation marks: "Reorder" and "OK".

ex4.title: Shipping Costs
ex4.story: Orders over 100 EUR get free shipping. Below 100 EUR shipping costs 5.99 EUR.
ex4.step1: Look at the table: column A contains order numbers, column B the order value.
ex4.step3: Copy the formula for C3 to C5
ex4.step4: Complete C6 with the IF formula as well
ex4.hint1: The comparison operator > means 'greater than'. Over 100 EUR = free shipping (0).
ex4.hint2: The IF function returns numbers here, not text. Therefore no quotation marks.
